title: Persistence Is Not Accumulation: Erasure, Suppression, and Re-instatement in Online In-Weight Memory for Language Models (instrument, results, and paper v1)
description: Instrument, raw per-fact timelines (86 runs), and paper v1 for a streaming audit of online in-weight memory in LLMs. Findings: (1) accumulation is re-instatement, not persistence — time-to-first-miss is ~3–8 writes for every mechanism while retention spans 12×, separated by recovery-after-miss (4–94%); (2) forgetting is erasure or suppression depending on the write protection (2-AFC recognition: naked SGD at chance, accumulated EWC ~84% latent); (3) error-gated replay converts suppressed facts to expressed ones at fixed budget (85–96% of a 48-fact day recallable from weights, capability firewall +0.00); (4) online fact writes are readout-proximal — the opposite of early-layer placement reported for batch knowledge injection. Single substrate (Qwen3.5-2B), 3 seeds per condition, per-seed numbers throughout.
